interactions.final_answer:
## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (complete end-to-end) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG **Scope name:** IKEA Global Co-worker Hub **Scope description:**

A single place for IKEA co-workers to find practical updates, policies, and ways of working across Ingka Group and Inter IKEA.

**Welcome message:**

Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find the information you need for a better everyday life—for the many people—plus the policies and guides that keep our way of working clear and consistent.

**Scope hero brand logo (Phase 1.5 upload):** https://img.logo.dev/ikea.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true **Brand colors (Diggspace mapping):** - **Primary:** #0058AB (IKEA Blue) - **Primary contrast:** #FFFFFF - **Secondary:** #FBD914 (IKEA Yellow) - **Warn:** #E00751 (Alert Red) - **Warn contrast:** #FFFFFF **Highlights (hub):** - number_of_highlights: **4** - highlights_carousel: **true** - highlights_style: **BannerCarousel** **Footer: 3 columns (real URLs from profile):** 1) **About & Careers** - about.ikea.com - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - https://www.ikeafoundation.org 2) **Performance & Reports** - https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ 3) **Support & Sustainability** - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ **Social links (global handles from profile):** - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ikea/ - Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ikea/ - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/ikea - X/Twitter: https://twitter.com/IKEA **navigation_links:** - **topBarLinks (3):** - https://about.ikea.com - https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - **footerLinks (5):** - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ - https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ - https://www.ikeafoundation.org **components (choose 3 based on profile priorities):** - **birthdays** (co-worker celebrations) - **workAnniversaries** (tenure + Tack!-style recognition) - **upcomingEvents** (town halls + manager briefings feed) --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels) Rules applied: - **3 primary channels** = **is_sticky: true** - **2 secondary channels** = **hide_highlights: true** - Utility channels hide from homepage feed only when appropriate - FAQs: included for HR/Legal/Compliance/IT/policy channels #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — “IKEA Inside: Operational Highlights” - **Purpose:** Timely, factual updates about business performance, product launches, and store changes. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #0058AB - **is_sticky:** true - **image_url (stock match):** stock/financial_report.png - **FAQ (2-3):** - Q: “Where do I find the latest information about store openings and formats?” A:

Start in IKEA Inside: Operational Highlights. Each update includes what changed, where it applies, and links for follow-up reading.

- Q: “How do price-reduction changes show up for co-workers and customers?” A:

We publish what was changed and the business context. For guidance on customer questions, check the relevant FAQ in Customer-facing Wayfinding and the linked policy items.

- Q: “Who updates these posts?” A:

Operational authors publish updates for the many people—using simple facts, dates, and clear next steps.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** false - **hide_highlights:** false - **Description (HTML):**

Operational updates written the IKEA way: short, concrete, and honest about what it means for co-workers on the ground. You’ll find business performance context, launch notes, and store-format changes.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** Reader #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — “People & Belonging” - **Purpose:** HR programs, learning, wellbeing, and co-worker development—translated into what changes for you. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #FBD914 - **is_sticky:** true - **image_url (stock match):** stock/career.png - **FAQ (2-3):** - Q: “What does ‘One IKEA Bonus’ mean for my unit?” A:

It’s a performance bonus based on unit-level performance—not individual-only metrics. Co-workers in the same unit share the same goal.

- Q: “How does onboarding work at IKEA?” A:

Onboarding blends on-the-job learning with classroom training, plus ongoing digital learning through the internal training platform.

- Q: “What is Tack!?” A:

Tack! is a pension contribution programme for eligible co-workers. The contribution amount is the same for eligible co-workers to reinforce solidarity.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** false - **hide_highlights:** false - **Description (HTML):**

People and growth at IKEA are built around togetherness and simple, practical learning. Find program notes, timelines, and what you can do next.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** Reader #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — “Democratic Design & Sustainability” - **Purpose:** Design philosophy, product development updates, and sustainability progress. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #0058AB - **is_sticky:** true - **image_url (stock match):** stock/sustainability.png - **FAQ (2-3):** - Q: “What is Democratic Design, and how do we measure it?” A:

Every product must meet Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, and Low Price. If a design can’t reach affordability, it fails Democratic Design.

- Q: “What renewable energy progress did IKEA report?” A:

FY25 reported 94.8% of operations matched with renewable electricity, against a goal of 100% by 2025.

- Q: “What is the circularity update on second-hand?” A:

Ingka Group launched a second-hand marketplace in Sweden in January 2026, with plans to expand to Spain, Norway, Portugal, and Poland.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** false - **hide_highlights:** false - **Description (HTML):**

Our mission is a better everyday life for the many people. This channel connects Democratic Design—designed from the price target—with sustainability progress and circularity in practice.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** Reader #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — “Policies, Security & Co-worker Responsibilities” - **Purpose:** Security, compliance, and mandatory policies that require explicit acknowledgement. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #E00751 - **is_sticky:** false - **image_url (stock match):** stock/security.png - **FAQ (2-3):** - Q: “When do I need to acknowledge a policy with ‘I have read this’?” A:

For items marked as policy/security/compliance, you’ll be required to acknowledge reading before proceeding.

- Q: “How do I report suspicious activity?” A:

Use your local security reporting path. If you’re unsure, start with the Security Basics guide in this channel.

- Q: “Where are the expectations for passwords?” A:

They’re covered in the password guidance. The rule is simple: don’t share, don’t reuse, and follow the secure reset process.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** true - **hide_highlights:** true - **Description (HTML):**

This channel is for mandatory co-worker responsibilities. If something is marked for acknowledgement, treat it like part of your job—clear, practical, and time-bound.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** None #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — “IT & Information: Practical Guides” - **Purpose:** How-to guidance for everyday work—learning, digital tools, and safer ways of working. - **Type:** private - **Color:** #0058AB - **is_sticky:** false - **image_url (stock match):** stock/Password.png - **frequent_questions:** [] (FAQ not required beyond policy/compliance; keep guides clean) - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** true - **hide_highlights:** true - **Description (HTML):**

Short, practical guides for co-workers: from secure access to information handling. If your day-to-day depends on digital tools, this is where to start.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** None --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (published; real dates; 1-2 events total) **Events planned:** 2 total - One town hall style event (end-to-end operational context) - One product / store-format learning session event (design + sustainability) **is_main_highlight set to exactly 4 items total across the hub.** **Pinned (is_sticky): 1-2 items per channel.** Stock image assignment for content uses the closest filename theme (from your list). If a channel image is already used, content may reuse another stock image. --- #### Ch1 — IKEA Inside: Operational Highlights (PRIMARY, 3 items) 1) **Title:** Prices down again: what FY25 price reductions meant for co-workers and customers - Type: announcement - tags: ['prices', 'fy25', 'many-people', 'operations'] - is_main_highlight: **true** - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: stock/financial_report.png - body outline: recap the FY24–FY25 price-reduction approach and how profit recovered while revenue declined. 2) **Title:** Oxford Street store (London) — the city-format prototype now in practice - Type: spotlight - tags: ['store-format', 'oxford-street', 'urban-strategy', 'operations'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/office.png - body outline: describe the May 2025 opening: 82,000 sq ft, six floors, electric heating, Swedish deli/restaurant, As-Is section. 3) **Title:** CEO transitions land: Juvencio Maeztu and Jakub Jankowski, with one clear mandate - Type: recap - tags: ['leadership', 'ceo', 'ingka', 'inter-ikea'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/leadership_speech.png - body outline: summarize the leadership changes (Nov 2025 and Jan 2026) and what it signals culturally. --- #### Ch2 — People & Belonging (PRIMARY, 3 items) 1) **Title:** Tack! and solidarity: how pension contributions work across co-worker roles - Type: guide - tags: ['tack', 'benefits', 'co-workers', 'understanding'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/community.png - body outline: explain Tack! and the “same contribution for eligible co-workers” principle. 2) **Title:** One IKEA Bonus explained: unit goals, not individual-only scorecards - Type: guide - tags: ['one-ikea-bonus', 'performance', 'togetherness', 'culture'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/team_diversity.png - body outline: describe unit-level bonus mechanics and why it reinforces shared goals. 3) **Title:** Restructuring 2026: what the 800 + 850 role changes mean for ways of working - Type: policy - tags: ['restructuring', 'faster-decisions', 'group-functions', 'clarity'] - is_main_highlight: **true** - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: true - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: stock/decision.png - body outline: cover the March/May 2026 cuts and hiring ~500 for new store formats; emphasize faster, shorter decision-making. --- #### Ch3 — Democratic Design & Sustainability (PRIMARY, 3 items) 1) **Title:** Renewable electricity milestone: FY25 hits 94.8% matched renewable operations - Type: milestone - tags: ['renewables', 'fy25', 'sustainability', 'targets'] - is_main_highlight: **true** - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/sustainability.png - body outline: report 94.8% vs 100% goal, and €4.2B invested/committed plus €3.3B planned through 2030. 2) **Title:** Second-hand marketplace (Sweden, Jan 2026): moving circularity from sections to systems - Type: spotlight - tags: ['circularity', 'second-hand', 'sweden', 'digital'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/Idea.png - body outline: explain why it matters commercially and culturally, and expansion plans (Spain, Norway, Portugal, Poland). 3) **Title:** Design backwards from the price: how Democratic Design kept Low Price inside every solution - Type: guide - tags: ['democratic-design', 'product', 'quality', 'low-price'] - is_main_highlight: **true** - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/QualityCheck.png - body outline: connect the 5 dimensions (Form/Function/Quality/Sustainability/Low Price) to how teams actually work. --- #### Ch4 — Policies, Security & Co-worker Responsibilities (SECONDARY, 2 items) 1) **Title:** Security basics for co-workers: passwords, phishing awareness, and safe sharing - Type: policy - tags: ['security', 'passwords', 'phishing', 'responsibility'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: true - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: stock/Password.png - body outline: practical rules; reference password guidance and “don’t share credentials”; include acknowledgement CTA. 2) **Title:** Restructuring acknowledgement: follow the revised decision-making and escalation paths - Type: policy - tags: ['compliance', 'decision-making', 'responsibilities', '2026'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: true - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/audit.png - body outline: document expected behaviours and where to find escalation routes. --- #### Ch5 — IT & Information: Practical Guides (SECONDARY, 2 items; includes 1 event) 1) **Title:** Flat Pack TV goes digital: how to find episode updates and co-worker stories - Type: recap - tags: ['learning', 'content', 'flat-pack-tv', 'stories'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/DigitalContent.png - body outline: how co-workers should locate updates and submit/track story contributions. 2) **Title (EVENT): “Lets Co-Report” Town Hall — bring your story, ask your questions - Type: event - tags: ['town-hall', 'let-co-report', 'tillsammans', 'qna'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: stock/video_call.png - **event_start:** 2026-06-18T10:00:00Z - **event_end:** 2026-06-18T11:00:00Z - location: “Online (live session with open Q&A)” - body outline: what to expect, how open Q&A works, and how to bring a story. *(We already used the second Event requirement? We have only 1 event so far. Need 1 more event total.)* ✅ Add one more event (still within Ch1 or Ch3—prefer Ch1 operational): #### Ch1 — add a second event item? We already set Ch1 to 3 items. Instead: replace one Ch1 item with an event while keeping item count at 3. - Replace Ch1 item #2 with event: **Ch1 item #2 (EVENT) update:** 2) **Title (EVENT): IKEA Inside Live — store-format learning session (Oxford Street & beyond)** - Type: event - tags: ['store-format', 'oxford-street', 'learning', 'operations'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: stock/warehouse.png - event_start: 2026-05-30T14:00:00Z - event_end: 2026-05-30T15:00:00Z - location: “Online + regional store cameras” - body outline: walkthrough of the city-format prototype features and what co-workers should test in their own contexts. --- ### 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (global settings) - **company_name:** IKEA - **website_name:** IKEA - **default_language:** en-US (inferred from global base + profile language defaults; unless you tell me HQ preference) - **supported_languages:** ['en-US'] (since profile doesn’t list explicit supported language codes; if you have them, I’ll swap them in) - **login_quick_links (5 from profile):** 1. https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ 2. https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ 3. https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ 4. https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ 5. https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - **sidebar_navigation (ordered priorities):** 1. **workgroups** (Togetherness + co-worker collaboration across concepts) 2. **documents** (policies + responsibilities) 3. **learning** (onboarding + internal learning platform) 4. **collaborators** (frontline stories / co-report spirit) 5. **apps** (digital tools entry points; “Let’s Co-Report” workflows) 6. **approvalRequests** (policy acknowledgement actions) 7. **ideation** (story + improvement channels) --- ## Request for approval (STOP) This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — channel names, content plan, colors, or hub settings?

arguments.name: IKEA Global Co-worker Hub
arguments.description: A single place for IKEA co-workers to find practical updates, policies, and ways of working across Ingka Group and Inter IKEA.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find the information you need for a better everyday life—for the many people—plus the policies and guides that keep our way of working clear and consistent.

channels.name: IKEA Inside: Operational Highlights
channels.description: Operational updates written the IKEA way: short, concrete, and honest about what it means for co-workers on the ground. You’ll find business performance context, launch notes, and store-format changes.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the latest information about store openings and formats?
frequent_questions.answer: Start in IKEA Inside: Operational Highlights. Each update includes what changed, where it applies, and links for follow-up reading.

frequent_questions.question: How do price-reduction changes show up for co-workers and customers?
frequent_questions.answer: We publish what was changed and the business context. For guidance on customer questions, check the relevant FAQ in Customer-facing Wayfinding and the linked policy items.

frequent_questions.question: Who updates these posts?
frequent_questions.answer: Operational authors publish updates for the many people—using simple facts, dates, and clear next steps.

channels.name: People & Belonging
channels.description: People and growth at IKEA are built around togetherness and simple, practical learning. Find program notes, timelines, and what you can do next.

frequent_questions.question: What does ‘One IKEA Bonus’ mean for my unit?
frequent_questions.answer: It’s a performance bonus based on unit-level performance—not individual-only metrics. Co-workers in the same unit share the same goal, reinforcing the team-over-individual ethic.

frequent_questions.question: How does onboarding work at IKEA?
frequent_questions.answer: Onboarding blends on-the-job learning with classroom training, plus ongoing digital learning through the internal training platform.

frequent_questions.question: What is Tack!?
frequent_questions.answer: Tack! is a pension contribution programme for eligible co-workers. The contribution amount is the same for eligible co-workers to reinforce solidarity and equality.

channels.name: Democratic Design & Sustainability
channels.description: Our mission is a better everyday life for the many people. This channel connects Democratic Design—designed from the price target—with sustainability progress and circularity in practice.

frequent_questions.question: What is Democratic Design, and how do we measure it?
frequent_questions.answer: Every product must meet Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, and Low Price. If a design can’t reach affordability, it fails Democratic Design.

frequent_questions.question: What renewable energy progress did IKEA report?
frequent_questions.answer: FY25 reported 94.8% of operations matched with renewable electricity, against a goal of 100% by 2025.

frequent_questions.question: What is the circularity update on second-hand?
frequent_questions.answer: Ingka Group launched a second-hand marketplace for IKEA products in Sweden in January 2026, with plans to expand to Spain, Norway, Portugal, and Poland.

channels.name: Policies, Security & Co-worker Responsibilities
channels.description: This channel is for mandatory co-worker responsibilities. If something is marked for acknowledgement, treat it like part of your job—clear, practical, and time-bound.

frequent_questions.question: When do I need to acknowledge a policy with ‘I have read this’?
frequent_questions.answer: For items marked as policy/security/compliance, you’ll be required to acknowledge reading before proceeding.

frequent_questions.question: How do I report suspicious activity?
frequent_questions.answer: Use your local security reporting path. If you’re unsure, start with the Security Basics guide in this channel.

frequent_questions.question: Where are the expectations for passwords?
frequent_questions.answer: They’re covered in the password guidance. The rule is simple: don’t share, don’t reuse, and follow the secure reset process.

channels.name: IT & Information: Practical Guides
channels.description: Short, practical guides for co-workers: from secure access to information handling. If your day-to-day depends on digital tools, this is where to start.
